notes: Detached SO(3) + re-voxelize regression suite. Shots 1-5 are the detached SO(3) checks (#1444 P5): (1) so3_smooth_sweep — primary smooth-deformation at cardinal yaw; (2) so3_offsnap_disc — per-voxel depth ordering at 45° off-cardinal yaw; (3) so3_wide_parity — full-scene cross-host GL/Metal parity; (4) so3_grid_cross — GRID-mode entities unaffected by DETACHED changes; (5) so3_offsnap_wide — second cross-host parity angle at 30°. Shot 6 is the detached re-voxelize path (epic #1553, baselined at P5 #1559): (6) revoxelize_solids — the asymmetric L-prism + cube re-voxelize proof solids at zoom 1 (headline 'reads as true-3D' criterion, #1555). The demo also emits a 7th shot, revoxelize_solids_zoom (zoom 2.2), which is intentionally EXCLUDED from this regression manifest: the 2.2x magnification amplifies the documented round-to-cell aliasing speckle (sub-cell CPU<->GPU float divergence on the aliased solid, expected per P1/P3) past the 99.9% match gate run-to-run (max_delta stays ~17, well under threshold), so it is non-deterministic by construction. It remains a manual/attach-screenshots inspection aid, not an automated check. The macos-debug baselines were regenerated at P5 to include the P4 AO/sun/light integration (#1558) that lights the re-voxelize detached canvases — the central cubes are lit; the peripheral forward-scatter ring is unchanged. warmup=120 matches the P4 Metal verification screenshot warmup (#1465). Identity fast-path (zero-rotation) is validated separately: run with --no-spin to verify the no-rotation path is byte-identical to master. Shot order mirrors g_allShots in main.cpp (kShots[] then the two appended re-voxelize shots) — the Nth entry maps to the Nth full-frame screenshot_NNNNNN.png. Reference PNGs live in sibling subdirs per CMake preset (macos-debug/, linux-debug/) since Metal and OpenGL produce pixel-different output; linux-debug/ is captured on a Linux/OpenGL host (fleet:needs-linux-smoke). The demo also emits two structural-validation shots beyond this pixel-diff set (T-1 / #1767, epic #1766): shadow_overlay_floor (capture with --debug-overlay shadow for render-shadow-metric.py #1765) and revox_coverage (re-voxelize surface coverage). They are appended after this manifest's shots so render-verify ignores them; their committed structural baselines + the reproduces/does-not-reproduce verdict live in ../baselines/. Wiring these into the gate (ROI-crop + structural thresholds) landed in T-2 (#1768) — see OPTIONAL GATE BLOCKS below. OPTIONAL GATE BLOCKS (epic #1766 T-2; both additive — omit for the original full-frame-only behavior): 'crops' = {shotLabel: [cropLabel, ...]} pixel-diffs each declared ROI crop (captured screenshot_NNNNNN_<shotLabel>__crop_<cropLabel>.png) against a committed reference crop <shotLabel>__crop_<cropLabel>.png in the backend dir, using the same 'thresholds'. 'structural' = {shotLabel: [{metric, roi?, <threshold>...}]} runs the backend-agnostic render-<metric>-metric.py gate on that shot's full-frame capture — e.g. {metric: 'shadow', roi: [x,y,w,h], max_hole_ratio: 0.05, max_components: 8} invokes render-shadow-metric.py (#1765); threshold keys map to CLI flags by key.replace('_','-'), and each gate needs at least one threshold. One structural threshold is a shared oracle across macos-debug + linux-debug (it measures structure, not pixels), so it can gate the otherwise-excluded revoxelize_solids_zoom shot once T-1 adds a SHADOW/coverage capture. 'shadow' is the only metric implemented today; coverage/silhouette/clip land in T-3. SECOND CAPTURE PASS (#1970; 'extra_runs', additive — omit for single-pass behavior): the unified-rotation comparison shots compare_yaw0 / compare_yaw_q (#1374) only spawn under --only compare (kGroupCompare, opt-in), which suppresses the default scene groups, so they cannot share the default capture. The 'extra_runs' entry {name:'compare', demo_args:['--only','compare'], shots:[compare_yaw0,compare_yaw_q], capture_offset:-3} re-runs the demo with --only compare and pixel-diffs those two shots against macos-debug/linux-debug references in the same backend dir. The --only compare run still emits the full default shot list (the leading frames render a near-empty scene) and appends the three compare shots (compare_yaw0, compare_yaw_q, compare_detached) at the tail, so capture_offset:-3 maps the first gated shot to the 3rd-from-last capture (compare_yaw0), leaving compare_detached ungated; negative offset stays correct as leading default shots are added/removed. compare_detached (half-pi yaw) is intentionally NOT gated (scope is yaw0/yaw_q; a mis-mapped slice would FAIL the pixel-diff loudly, not silently pass). These references are blessed per-host (macos-debug here; linux-debug ships separately like the #1944/#1969 main-set split), so a backend without them committed SKIPS the compare pass instead of failing — run render-verify --update-references on that host to engage the gate there. FLOOR SELF-SHADOW GUARD (#2092, 'floor_selfshadow' extra_run — structural_only, no reference PNG): the SDF floor self-shadow acne (#1717 C-series receive-side facet) was fixed by C3 (#2275, shared sun-projection for cast+receive); this positive gate keeps it fixed while #2270 bake-splat / #2207 polarity-carrier churn the exact bake + distance-encoding shaders. It re-runs '--only floor --debug-overlay shadow' (zero casters), slices the so3_offsnap_wide capture (index 4 = yaw pi/6, the acne-classic within-sun-texel-spread pose), and asserts render-shadow-metric.py --min-hole-ratio 0.98 — a fully-lit floor reads hole_ratio ~1.0 (measured 1.0, shadow_px=0 on master), and a self-shadow regression (pre-fix: entire floor read magenta) drives it toward 0. Backend-agnostic structural oracle (structure, not pixels), so one 0.98 threshold gates both macos-debug + linux-debug with no committed reference. capture_offset 4 is head-stable: so3_offsnap_wide is the last of the 5 unconditional kShots[] assigned first in g_allShots, and --only isolates entity groups without changing the shot list. SHADOW COVERAGE GATE (#2270, 'shadow_overlay_floor' extra_run — structural_only, no reference PNG): the moth-eaten cast-shadow point-scatter (#1717) was fixed by the bounded atomicMin coverage-splat box (docs/design/sun-shadow-bake-coverage.md); this gate keeps the fix in place across future bake churn (e.g. the #2270 structural-splat-confinement fix, #2207 polarity carrier). It re-runs '--no-auto-rotate --no-spin --debug-overlay shadow' (default scene frozen at cardinal — the deterministic pose the design-doc oracle measured; avoids the auto-rotate+self-spin non-determinism the metric has under motion) and slices g_allShots index 8 = shadow_overlay_floor (5 kShots[] + revoxelize_solids + revoxelize_solids_zoom + orbit_overview), asserting render-shadow-metric.py --roi 1010,540,450,250 --max-components 8 --min-largest-frac 0.9 (measured 1 comp / largest_frac 1.0 at r6). A coverage regression (splat off / mis-radius) shatters the cast shadow into many small components (pre-fix: 58 comp / 0.306 largest_frac), driving components up and largest_frac down. Backend-agnostic structural oracle, so one threshold gates both macos-debug + linux-debug with no committed reference; a saturated (already-dense) host reads even fewer components, so it stays inside the bound. capture_offset 8 is head-stable: the 8 leading shots are all unconditional (the compare shots are opt-in via --only compare, absent here), and --no-auto-rotate/--no-spin do not change the shot list.
